Acceptance: triage-ready artifacts for every failure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:31.390364301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:56:41.913463820Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:56:41.913434796Z","close_reason":"Completed: added integrated extension E2E runner with structured scenario diagnostics and mismatch digests. New script: e2e/extension_integrated_wave_report.sh; inventory wired in crates/fsqlite-harness/src/e2e_traceability.rs. Evidence run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-plumbeaver-f bash e2e/extension_integrated_wave_report.sh --json -> run_id=bd-1dp9.5.4-20260213T085610Z-3490834 report=test-results/bd_1dp9_5_4/bd-1dp9.5.4-20260213T085610Z-3490834.jsonl sha256=848014d1d475003b1e1aa5f06951e7de47a4f0ee380311c7d7005a11a039b0e3, failures=0. Formatting/syntax: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/fsqlite-harness/src/e2e_traceability.rs && bash -n e2e/extension_integrated_wave_report.sh.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","extensions","logging","parity100"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":224,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Mandatory Verification Contract (applies to this bead)

This task is not complete until evidence is attached for all three layers:
1. **Unit tests**: deterministic tests proving local semantics/invariants for the changed surface.
2. **E2E scripts**: scenario-level scripts exercising realistic flows and differential behavior where applicable.
3. **Structured logging artifacts**: machine-parseable logs with run_id, seed/config, phase markers, first-divergence indicators (when relevant), and artifact hashes.

Minimum evidence bundle:
- test identifiers + pass/fail summary,
- reproduction command(s),
- output artifact locations/hashes,
- short rationale linking evidence to acceptance criteria.

Gate policy:
- Missing any one of the three layers = bead remains open.
- Evidence must be reproducible on a clean checkout with pinned config/seeds.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6","title":"Track 6: Extreme Optimization and Performance Parity","description":"## Context
Parity without performance is unacceptable; optimization without proof is dangerous.

## Goal
Achieve performance parity targets using strict profile-first, isomorphism-preserving optimization loops.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Baselines + flamegraphs + opportunity matrix published.
- Only score>=2.0 optimizations implemented.
- Every optimization has isomorphism proof + golden checksum verification.
- Final perf parity report includes p50/p95/p99 and throughput deltas.

## Success Criteria
Epic completion requires all child beads to satisfy their structured acceptance criteria, including explicit unit/property coverage, deterministic e2e evidence, and structured logging/metrics validation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every child bead in this epic defines concrete unit/property test scope with deterministic pass/fail thresholds and invariant IDs. 2. Critical-path end-to-end scenarios are implemented as reproducible e2e scripts (fixed seeds, replay commands, artifact bundles) across happy-path and adversarial-path behavior. 3. Structured logging and metrics contracts are mandatory for all child outputs (trace_id, run_id, scenario_id, seed, timing, outcome, error context) and validated by CI conformance checks. 4. Epic completion requires a machine-readable evidence manifest linking child unit/e2e/log artifacts to acceptance outcomes, plus an explicit fallback/rollback plan that preserves concurrent-writer defaults.","notes":"2026-02-23 WhiteKite follow-up: Updated acceptance contracts for bd-1dp9.6.2, bd-1dp9.6.4, bd-1dp9.9.2, bd-1dp9.9.3, bd-1dp9.9.4, bd-1dp9.9.5 plus remaining baseline regressions (bd-2qr3a, bd-2qr3a.5, bd-2y306.2, bd-2y306.5, bd-3bw.5, bd-3plop.5) so quality-gate parser requirements are explicit: unit/property tests, deterministic e2e fixed-seed replay, and structured logging/metrics with trace_id/run_id/scenario_id. Verification: target/debug/backlog_quality_gate_runner ... => overall_pass=true, total_failures=0, regression_failures=0; artifacts/backlog-quality-gate-e2e/repo/report-latest.{json,md}.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:23.366156385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T04:59:49.902520723Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","isomorphism","optimization","parity100","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":225,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 6 operating notes:
- Apply extreme optimization discipline without semantic drift.
- Optimization work is gated by profile evidence and behavior-preservation proof.

Design choices:
- Mandatory loop: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix -> single-lever change -> golden/isomorphism proof -> reprofile.
- Only execute opportunities with Impact*Confidence/Effort >= threshold defined in T6.1 policy.
- Keep SQL-path and storage/MVCC-path optimization streams separated for attribution clarity.

Test/logging requirements:
- Benchmark artifacts include p50/p95/p99, throughput, and memory deltas.
- Golden output checksum validation before/after each optimization set.
- Isomorphism proof notes attached per optimization change set.

Definition of done:
- Performance parity target met with explicit non-regression evidence and CI guardrails.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:11:25Z"}]}

Acceptance: only score>=2.0 opportunities promoted.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:31.734533691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:43:56.920755426Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:43:56.920736691Z","close_reason":"Completed: baseline benchmark pack runner + verifier + threshold-gated opportunity matrix with unit/e2e/log evidence attached","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","parity100","perf","profiling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":226,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Mandatory Verification Contract (applies to this bead)

Gate policy:

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:31Z"},{"id":227,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification evidence bundle for bd-1dp9.6.1 (baseline benchmark pack + profiling + opportunity matrix):

1) Unit tests (deterministic invariants)
- Command: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-silentwolf-1dp961 cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test perf_loop_enforcement -- --nocapture
- Result: PASS (35 passed, 0 failed)
- Includes acceptance guard: test_e2e_only_threshold_qualified_opportunities_promoted

2) E2E script (scenario-level)
- Command: ./scripts/verify_perf_baseline_pack.sh
- Successful runs recorded in structured log:
  - run_id=bd-1dp9.6.1-20260213T093157Z-319985 -> stage=runner complete + stage=complete verification succeeded
  - run_id=bd-1dp9.6.1-20260213T093452Z-375461 -> stage=runner complete + stage=complete verification succeeded
- Re-run attempt at 2026-02-13T09:41Z is currently blocked by unrelated concurrent-tree compile regressions in fsqlite-vdbe/vectorized.rs (outside this bead scope).

3) Structured logging + artifacts (machine-parseable)
- report: artifacts/perf/bd-1dp9.6.1/report.json
  - sha256=9c0b9f43d70094788859390c8023ac27e7b5566aa7b0548c6427c6c3e11845ca
  - bead_id=bd-1dp9.6.1 overall_pass=true scenario_count=7 promoted_count=6
- opportunity matrix: artifacts/perf/bd-1dp9.6.1/opportunity_matrix.json
  - sha256=6fb75ee9716b0c9ab3c720489af0a7ec208edb13a472f40946c53fbc89b27f24
  - threshold=2.0; promoted candidates all selected=true and score>=threshold; at least one non-promoted candidate present
- summary: artifacts/perf/bd-1dp9.6.1/summary.md
  - sha256=3c2a356f278666edd713325da7fa93499251de5222a319e07b507695dcef7779
- profiling artifact report: artifacts/perf/bd-1dp9.6.1/profiling_artifact_report.json
  - sha256=764506d87a6019dd4ace25a1342ed1f2fc4e94bac2a5b5ee5627e4e675ebc572
- baseline outputs:
  - baselines/criterion/bd-1dp9.6.1-baseline.json sha256=cda76a92e912f2c3062c07b3fcb486a7fce45d4bba68e59e817aabd2eb4ba710
  - baselines/smoke/bd-1dp9.6.1-smoke-report.json sha256=48162121960df0c4914f4e911c57fe525ce7d1a2c228bfa95f35410cb5e8823c
  - baselines/hyperfine/bd-1dp9.6.1-hyperfine.json sha256=849f61130424478788b19dedffce6215b896101ef754041b6a95af8d08faae13
- script log: artifacts/perf/bd-1dp9.6.1/verify_perf_baseline_pack.log
  - sha256=9de2cacb89dd2478502a3b4e9a84f7f37422c2cc8f3fd54a1a5e0df5f6da8a42

Acceptance rationale:
- Baseline benchmark pack and profiling artifacts are generated deterministically.
- Opportunity matrix gate enforces only score>=2.0 promotions, with below-threshold candidates retained as non-promoted.
- Unit + e2e + structured logging evidence are attached with reproducible commands and artifact hashes.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:43:49Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","title":"T6.2 SQL pipeline hotspot optimization sprints (single lever, proof-preserving)","description":"Run iterative optimization loops on parser/planner/VDBE hotspots with one lever per change. For each change include isomorphism proof and golden checksum verification. Acceptance: measurable gains with zero behavior drift.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is complete and behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures, fixed seeds, and regression fixtures. 3. Deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior in real execution flows and include fixed seeds plus replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics must include trace_id, run_id, scenario_id, timing/counter fields, and actionable failure context; CI must validate log schema conformance.","notes":"2026-02-23 WhiteKite: Claimed Track 6 SQL lane and normalized acceptance contract to include explicit structured logging identifiers (trace_id/run_id/scenario_id) plus fixed-seed deterministic E2E wording. Re-ran backlog quality gate runner with current repo baseline; track-6/track-9 regressions for bd-1dp9.* are cleared in artifacts/backlog-quality-gate-e2e/repo/report-latest.{json,md}. Remaining regressions are now limited to six non-track-6 issues (bd-2qr3a, bd-2qr3a.5, bd-2y306.2, bd-2y306.5, bd-3bw.5, bd-3plop.5).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkKnoll","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:32.086524332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T10:07:10.645526121Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["isomorphism","parity100","perf","sql-pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:42Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:42Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:42Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:42Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2184,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Mandatory Verification Contract (applies to this bead)

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:31Z"},{"id":2185,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed via bv robot triage as highest-impact startable optimization lane after closure of bd-1dp9.9.1. Starting with profile-guided hotspot selection in SQL pipeline (parser/planner/VDBE), then implementing exactly one optimization lever with behavior-preserving checks, deterministic unit/e2e evidence, and structured logs per acceptance contract.","created_at":"2026-02-20T01:46:11Z"},{"id":2186,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (2026-02-20): delivered first concrete optimization-governance slice for this bead. In crates/fsqlite-harness/src/sql_pipeline_optimization.rs, assessment now enforces a real SQL hotspot gate sourced from T6.1 opportunity matrix artifact (artifacts/perf/bd-1dp9.6.1/opportunity_matrix.json) instead of pure static checks, recording selected_sql_hotspots + matrix scenario/threshold in report output and failing parity if SQL hotspot selection/isomorphism gate does not hold. Added new unit tests for gate pass/fail fixtures and deterministic structured artifact emission (SQL_PIPELINE_OPT_ARTIFACT_JSON + SQL_PIPELINE_OPT_REPORT_JSON lines). Added deterministic e2e script e2e/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.sh with structured JSONL run report/logs and artifact extraction fallback for rch remote artifact locality. Validation: rch exec cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib sql_pipeline_optimization::tests:: -- --nocapture PASS (17 tests); ./e2e/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.sh --json PASS with run_id=bd-1dp9.6.2-20260220T020437Z-3913021 report_sha256=5bac2319218e050df1c9c5525bedda760bc5185833135f87404d757b9c3fb0f1. Workspace-wide rch cargo check/clippy currently blocked by pre-existing frankentui compile errors (outside bead files).","created_at":"2026-02-20T02:09:21Z"},{"id":2187,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"VioletEagle","text":"2026-03-03 VioletEagle progress slice (single-lever optimization):

Implemented parse-cache fast path and collision-safe matching in `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`.

What changed:
1) Added `ParseCacheEntry { sql, statement }` and changed cache type to `HashMap<u64, ParseCacheEntry>`.
2) Added helpers:
   - `refresh_parse_cache_if_needed()`
   - `lookup_parse_cache(key, sql)` (requires SQL equality, not just hash match)
   - `insert_parse_cache(key, sql, statement)`
3) Updated `cached_parse_multi()` to hit cache before parsing; repeated single-statement `execute()` calls now bypass parser on cache hit.
4) Added regression tests:
   - `test_parse_cache_execute_uses_single_statement_fast_path`
   - `test_parse_cache_hash_match_requires_sql_equality`

Verification evidence:
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` => PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` => PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib parse_cache -- --nocapture` => PASS (7 tests)
- `./e2e/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.sh --json` => PASS
  - run_id: `bd-1dp9.6.2-20260303T015822Z-3265557`
  - report: `test-results/bd_1dp9_6_2/bd-1dp9.6.2-20260303T015822Z-3265557.jsonl`
  - report_sha256: `cebcd1cd63e34749ac34a673b8dbb3325c681010626eb6531f64375975c161cb`

Notes:
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` currently fails due pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated files (`crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_3u7_4_vfs_contract_fault_injection.rs`, `crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs`).
","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:04:56Z"},{"id":2188,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-03-04 RusticGorge progress slice (single-lever SQL pipeline optimization)

Scope:
- `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`
- Added a fast-path precheck to skip statement-level subquery rewrite traversal when the statement has no rewrite-relevant EXISTS/scalar-subquery expressions.

Implementation details:
1. In `rewrite_subquery_statement`, added early return:
   - `if !statement_contains_rewritable_subquery(&statement) { return Ok(statement); }`
2. Added lightweight read-only detectors:
   - `statement_contains_rewritable_subquery`
   - `select_contains_rewritable_subquery`
   - `select_core_contains_rewritable_subquery`
   - `expr_contains_rewritable_subquery`
3. Added deterministic unit tests:
   - `test_statement_subquery_rewrite_probe_plain_select_is_false`
   - `test_statement_subquery_rewrite_probe_exists_is_true`
   - `test_statement_subquery_rewrite_probe_in_subquery_only_is_false`
   - `test_statement_subquery_rewrite_probe_update_scalar_subquery_is_true`

Evidence bundle:
- Unit tests (deterministic):
  - `rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib statement_subquery_rewrite_probe -- --nocapture`
  - Result: PASS (4 passed, 0 failed)
- Workspace compile gate:
  - `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
  - Result: PASS
- Workspace clippy gate:
  - `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - Result: PASS
- Formatting gate:
  - `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check`
  - Result: FAIL due pre-existing unrelated formatting drift in:
    - `crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs`
    - `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs`
  - No formatting diffs remained in this slice file.

E2E + structured logging artifacts:
- Command: `./e2e/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.sh --json`
- Status: PASS
- run_id: `bd-1dp9.6.2-20260304T193903Z-4015543`
- seed: `1091901001`
- report_jsonl: `test-results/bd_1dp9_6_2/bd-1dp9.6.2-20260304T193903Z-4015543.jsonl`
- report_sha256: `2b1f9548a3b034457fdf354de90f045382908bd221b7f1357a7a163fc818a609`
- artifact_path: `target/bd_1dp9_6_2_runtime/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.json`
- artifact_sha256: `f291742b0f642e2aac9b7bac65d9bf764e17ecc8dd9c56670a299e1b237207e2`

Acceptance-criteria linkage:
- Behavioral correctness: detector only gates traversal/rewrite invocation; rewrite semantics unchanged for statements requiring EXISTS/scalar-subquery rewrite.
- Unit coverage: explicit positive/negative detector tests added and passing.
- E2E deterministic evidence: run_id/seed and artifact hashes captured from script output.
- Structured logging: scenario/run identifiers emitted by script and persisted to report JSONL.
","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:54:30Z"},{"id":2417,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"codex","text":"2026-03-16 Codex audit: resetting from in_progress to open. This lane has real completed slices recorded on 2026-02-20, 2026-03-03, and 2026-03-04, but there is no close-quality evidence that the full T6.2 SQL-pipeline optimization lane is done, no current assignee, and no recent follow-through after those old slices. It should return to the ready pool instead of implying active ownership.","created_at":"2026-03-16T23:19:47Z"},{"id":2420,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"PinkKnoll","text":"2026-03-17 PinkKnoll claiming T6.2 for an executor-side parser/compile churn slice. Current implementation focus: normalize repeated literal integer point-select SQL in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fsqlite_executor.rs into one prepared statement shape with bound params so mixed_read_write-style workloads stop paying parse/compile tax for each literal variant. Reserved only crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fsqlite_executor.rs and explicitly staying out of crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs because RainyWren claimed bd-db300.2.2 there. Verification is currently blocked by pre-existing borrow-check failures in engine.rs, but targeted executor tests for the new reuse path have been added in this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:13:30Z"},{"id":2421,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"PinkKnoll","text":"2026-03-17 PinkKnoll progress: extended e2e/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.sh so the bead-specific replay path now runs the executor-side reuse tests for repeated literal point-select normalization ( and ).  passes for the script. Full cargo validation is still blocked by existing unrelated borrow-check failures in crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs, which belongs to RainyWren's bd-db300.2.2 slice, so this T6.2 slice remains intentionally isolated to the executor/evidence surface.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:15:37Z"},{"id":2422,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"PinkKnoll","text":"Correction to prior progress comment: the two executor-side tests added to the T6.2 replay path are named prepared_op_executor_normalizes_varying_point_selects and run_oplog_fsqlite_prepared_sql_reduces_parser_churn_for_varying_point_selects. The previous comment lost those names because the shell interpreted backticks during command construction.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:15:48Z"},{"id":2425,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"PinkKnoll","text":"2026-03-17 PinkKnoll progress: extended the executor-side normalization slice to repeated literal point-deletes. crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fsqlite_executor.rs now normalizes simple DELETE FROM <table> WHERE id = N statements into one prepared shape with bound id parameters, alongside the earlier point-select path. Cheap verification completed: cargo fmt on the touched Rust file and bash -n on the replay script.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:36:52Z"},{"id":2427,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"2026-03-17 PinkKnoll progress: extending the executor-side normalization slice from point-selects/point-deletes to strict raw UPDATE-by-id literals in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fsqlite_executor.rs. Current shape targeted: simple UPDATE <table> SET <simple-column>=<simple-literal>[, ...] WHERE id = <int> statements, specifically to collapse repeated migration/backfill-style SQL variants into one prepared statement shape and cut parser/compile churn on the replay path. Added matching executor-shape and parser-churn tests plus a new update-churn phase in e2e/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.sh; verification is in flight now via rch for the new targeted test, with local cargo fmt and bash -n already passing.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:58:31Z"},{"id":2429,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"2026-03-17 PinkKnoll progress: raw UPDATE-by-id literal normalization is now present in HEAD for the executor-side T6.2 lane. In crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fsqlite_executor.rs the reusable raw-SQL normalization path now folds strict simple UPDATE <table> SET <col>=<literal>[, ...] WHERE id = <int> statements into one prepared shape, including simple literal parsing for quoted text / numeric / NULL values; direct shape and parser-churn tests for varying updates are also present there. The bead replay script e2e/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.sh now includes both executor_update_shape_reuse and executor_update_churn_gate phases. Cheap verification passed locally: cargo fmt on the touched Rust file and bash -n on the replay script. Remote validation command launched successfully through rch on worker vmi1153651 (no fail-open fallback), but final test completion was still pending at time of this comment after the worker compiled through fsqlite-core/fsqlite-vdbe/fsqlite-harness and into fsqlite-e2e itself.","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:14:14Z"},{"id":2430,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"2026-03-17 Session 73 progress — concrete VDBE hot-path optimizations:

1. **Function/PureFunc zero-allocation args** (engine.rs ~7766): Direct register slice `&self.registers[start..end]` instead of `collect_reg_range()` SmallVec allocation+clone per scalar function call. Same pattern as AggStep.

2. **IdxGT/IdxLE SmallVec clone elimination** (engine.rs ~7380): Previous fix cloned `cur_vals_buf`+`target_vals_buf` into temp SmallVecs to satisfy borrow checker. Replaced with `Arc::clone` of collation registry (16-byte atomic) + direct reference to cursor buffers. Saves 2 SmallVec allocs + all-values clone per index comparison.

3. **Cross-crate `#[inline(always)]` for hot arithmetic** (value.rs): Upgraded `sql_add`, `sql_sub`, `sql_mul`, `is_null`, `to_integer`, `to_float` from `#[inline]` to `#[inline(always)]`. Guarantees inlining into VDBE dispatch loop across crate boundary.

4. **set_reg_int conversions**: BitAnd, BitOr, BitNot, AddImm, Not, comparison STOREP2 — all write integers and now skip NaN check + enum construction.

5. **Inlined set_reg/take_reg**: Added `#[inline]` to both methods called on nearly every opcode.

Verification: `cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe -p fsqlite-types` PASS. VDBE tests: 528 pass (32 pre-existing failures from other agents' concurrent work — same count as HEAD).

This task is not complete until evidence is attached for all three layers:
1. **Unit tests**: deterministic tests proving local semantics/invariants for the changed surface.
2. **E2E scripts**: scenario-level scripts exercising realistic flows and differential behavior where applicable.
3. **Structured logging artifacts**: machine-parseable logs with run_id, seed/config, phase markers, first-divergence indicators (when relevant), and artifact hashes.

Minimum evidence bundle:
- test identifiers + pass/fail summary,
- reproduction command(s),
- output artifact locations/hashes,
- short rationale linking evidence to acceptance criteria.

Gate policy:
- Missing any one of the three layers = bead remains open.
- Evidence must be reproducible on a clean checkout with pinned config/seeds.

Workspace-wide rch cargo check/clippy currently blocked by pre-existing frankentui compile errors (outside bead files).","created_at":"2026-02-20T02:09:21Z"},{"id":2187,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"VioletEagle","text":"2026-03-03 VioletEagle progress slice (single-lever optimization):

Implemented parse-cache fast path and collision-safe matching in `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`.

What changed:
1) Added `ParseCacheEntry { sql, statement }` and changed cache type to `HashMap<u64, ParseCacheEntry>`.
2) Added helpers:
   - `refresh_parse_cache_if_needed()`
   - `lookup_parse_cache(key, sql)` (requires SQL equality, not just hash match)
   - `insert_parse_cache(key, sql, statement)`
3) Updated `cached_parse_multi()` to hit cache before parsing; repeated single-statement `execute()` calls now bypass parser on cache hit.
4) Added regression tests:
   - `test_parse_cache_execute_uses_single_statement_fast_path`
   - `test_parse_cache_hash_match_requires_sql_equality`

Verification evidence:
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` => PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` => PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib parse_cache -- --nocapture` => PASS (7 tests)
- `./e2e/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.sh --json` => PASS
  - run_id: `bd-1dp9.6.2-20260303T015822Z-3265557`
  - report: `test-results/bd_1dp9_6_2/bd-1dp9.6.2-20260303T015822Z-3265557.jsonl`
  - report_sha256: `cebcd1cd63e34749ac34a673b8dbb3325c681010626eb6531f64375975c161cb`

Notes:
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` currently fails due pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated files (`crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_3u7_4_vfs_contract_fault_injection.rs`, `crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs`).
","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:04:56Z"},{"id":2188,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-03-04 RusticGorge progress slice (single-lever SQL pipeline optimization)

Scope:
- `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`
- Added a fast-path precheck to skip statement-level subquery rewrite traversal when the statement has no rewrite-relevant EXISTS/scalar-subquery expressions.

Implementation details:
1. In `rewrite_subquery_statement`, added early return:
   - `if !statement_contains_rewritable_subquery(&statement) { return Ok(statement); }`
2. Added lightweight read-only detectors:
   - `statement_contains_rewritable_subquery`
   - `select_contains_rewritable_subquery`
   - `select_core_contains_rewritable_subquery`
   - `expr_contains_rewritable_subquery`
3. Added deterministic unit tests:
   - `test_statement_subquery_rewrite_probe_plain_select_is_false`
   - `test_statement_subquery_rewrite_probe_exists_is_true`
   - `test_statement_subquery_rewrite_probe_in_subquery_only_is_false`
   - `test_statement_subquery_rewrite_probe_update_scalar_subquery_is_true`

Evidence bundle:
- Unit tests (deterministic):
  - `rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib statement_subquery_rewrite_probe -- --nocapture`
  - Result: PASS (4 passed, 0 failed)
- Workspace compile gate:
  - `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
  - Result: PASS
- Workspace clippy gate:
  - `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - Result: PASS
- Formatting gate:
  - `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check`
  - Result: FAIL due pre-existing unrelated formatting drift in:
    - `crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs`
    - `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs`
  - No formatting diffs remained in this slice file.

E2E + structured logging artifacts:
- Command: `./e2e/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.sh --json`
- Status: PASS
- run_id: `bd-1dp9.6.2-20260304T193903Z-4015543`
- seed: `1091901001`
- report_jsonl: `test-results/bd_1dp9_6_2/bd-1dp9.6.2-20260304T193903Z-4015543.jsonl`
- report_sha256: `2b1f9548a3b034457fdf354de90f045382908bd221b7f1357a7a163fc818a609`
- artifact_path: `target/bd_1dp9_6_2_runtime/bd_1dp9_6_2_sql_pipeline_optimization_report.json`
- artifact_sha256: `f291742b0f642e2aac9b7bac65d9bf764e17ecc8dd9c56670a299e1b237207e2`

Acceptance-criteria linkage:
- Behavioral correctness: detector only gates traversal/rewrite invocation; rewrite semantics unchanged for statements requiring EXISTS/scalar-subquery rewrite.
- Unit coverage: explicit positive/negative detector tests added and passing.
- E2E deterministic evidence: run_id/seed and artifact hashes captured from script output.
- Structured logging: scenario/run identifiers emitted by script and persisted to report JSONL.

1. **Function/PureFunc zero-allocation args** (engine.rs ~7766): Direct register slice `&self.registers[start..end]` instead of `collect_reg_range()` SmallVec allocation+clone per scalar function call. Same pattern as AggStep.

2. **IdxGT/IdxLE SmallVec clone elimination** (engine.rs ~7380): Previous fix cloned `cur_vals_buf`+`target_vals_buf` into temp SmallVecs to satisfy borrow checker. Replaced with `Arc::clone` of collation registry (16-byte atomic) + direct reference to cursor buffers. Saves 2 SmallVec allocs + all-values clone per index comparison.

3. **Cross-crate `#[inline(always)]` for hot arithmetic** (value.rs): Upgraded `sql_add`, `sql_sub`, `sql_mul`, `is_null`, `to_integer`, `to_float` from `#[inline]` to `#[inline(always)]`. Guarantees inlining into VDBE dispatch loop across crate boundary.

4. **set_reg_int conversions**: BitAnd, BitOr, BitNot, AddImm, Not, comparison STOREP2 — all write integers and now skip NaN check + enum construction.

5. **Inlined set_reg/take_reg**: Added `#[inline]` to both methods called on nearly every opcode.

","created_at":"2026-03-17T08:44:26Z"},{"id":2432,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Hash-join fast path for equi-joins added to execute_single_join. O(n+m) vs O(n*m). Also skip column default eval for non-INSERT VDBE programs. 926 oracle_ext tests pass, 0 regressions.","created_at":"2026-03-17T10:07:10Z"},{"id":2434,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"2026-03-17 Session 73b — MemDatabase direct-read JOIN fast path

Implemented in `execute_join_select` (connection.rs ~21426): For `:memory:` databases, table scans in JOIN paths now read directly from MemDatabase instead of going through the full `self.query()` pipeline (parse→compile→VDBE→autocommit per table).

Before: Every table in a JOIN called `self.query(build_join_scan_sql(src))` which re-parses and re-compiles a `SELECT * FROM table` through the entire statement execution pipeline.

After: When `!self.pager.is_file_backed()`, reads directly from `MemDatabase.iter_rows()` with IPK column splicing and hidden rowid projection — same logic as the existing time-travel path.

Impact: Eliminates parse+compile+VDBE overhead per table in JOIN. For a 2-table JOIN on :memory:, this removes 2 full statement execution cycles per JOIN evaluation. Previous attempt by another agent reverted due to "column format differences" — this implementation copies the exact IPK-splice logic from the time-travel path to ensure matching column formats.

Note: Currently blocked from compilation by another agent's incomplete Arc<str>/AST refactor in fsqlite-types.
","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:50:46Z"}]}

Baseline (before lever): incoming active_readers/4 1.56-1.60us, /8 3.44-3.47us, /16 6.67-6.74us; outgoing committed_writers/4 1.48-1.49us, /8 3.17-3.25us, /16 6.11-6.18us. Lever tested: replace per-call page index structure in discover_incoming_edges/discover_outgoing_edges from BTreeMap to HashMap with preallocation. Result: statistically significant regression across all sizes (roughly +21% to +52% latency). Change was fully reverted; codebase left with no performance-regressing behavior changes. Validation after revert: cargo fmt --check -p fsqlite-mvcc; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-quietwillow-mvcc cargo clippy -p fsqlite-mvcc --all-targets -- -D warnings; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-quietwillow-mvcc cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc ssi_validation::tests::test_ -- --nocapture; Mandatory Verification Contract (applies to this bead)

This task is not complete until evidence is attached for all three layers:
1. **Unit tests**: deterministic tests proving local semantics/invariants for the changed surface.
2. **E2E scripts**: scenario-level scripts exercising realistic flows and differential behavior where applicable.
3. **Structured logging artifacts**: machine-parseable logs with run_id, seed/config, phase markers, first-divergence indicators (when relevant), and artifact hashes.

Minimum evidence bundle:
- test identifiers + pass/fail summary,
- reproduction command(s),
- output artifact locations/hashes,
- short rationale linking evidence to acceptance criteria.

Gate policy:
- Missing any one of the three layers = bead remains open.
- Evidence must be reproducible on a clean checkout with pinned config/seeds.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.5","title":"T6.5 Isomorphism-proof harness and golden checksum automation for optimization changes","description":"Operationalize optimization safety: every performance change must carry proof that behavior is preserved. ","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:32Z"},{"id":239,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-1dp9.6.5 Closure Evidence: Isomorphism-proof harness and golden checksum automation

### Summary
Full parity across all 10 proof invariant classes, including all 4 mandatory invariants (golden_checksum, error_codes, null_propagation, aggregate_semantics). SHA-256 golden checksum automation with CI gate enforcement.

### Implementation
- crates/fsqlite-harness/src/isomorphism_proof.rs (orchestrator module)
  - ProofInvariantClass enum (10 classes: row_ordering, tie_break, floating_point_precision, rng_determinism, golden_checksum, type_affinity, null_propagation, error_codes, aggregate_semantics, window_function_semantics)
  - IsomorphismVerdict (Parity, Partial, Drift)
  - IsomorphismProofRecord for machine-readable proof records
  - 20 individual checks across 10 invariant classes
  - assess_isomorphism_proof() orchestrator + file I/O + triage_line
  - 17 unit tests

### Evidence
1) Harness unit (17p/0f): cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib -- isomorphism_proof
2) Harness integration (11p/0f): cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_1dp9_6_5_isomorphism_proof
3) Toolchain determinism (58p/0f): cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib -- toolchain_determinism
4) Ratchet policy (25p/0f): cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib -- ratchet_policy
5) E2E script: PASS (111 total, 0 failed). Artifact: test-results/bd-1dp9.6.5-isomorphism-proof-verify.json
","created_at":"2026-02-13T20:34:59Z"}]}
